qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to submit a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: To be eligible for a RAC application, the Principal Investigator (PI) and all Co-PIs must be faculty members at a Canadian academic institution and have an active Alliance account in the CCDB with an Academic PI role (Faculty, Adjunct Faculty, or Librarian). Additionally, PIs must apply with their primary, most up-to-date position, and 'Departmental' applications are not accepted.

qa_pairs.question: What are the minimum resource amounts required for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application related to CPU and GPU on High-Performance Computing (HPC) clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: For CPU resources on HPC clusters, a request must be equal to or greater than 200 core-years or core-equivalent-years in each cluster where these resources are needed. For GPU resources, a request must be equal to or greater than 25 reference GPU units (RGU) years in each cluster where these resources are required.

qa_pairs.question: In the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), what is the difference between a Co-PI and a Collaborator, and what are the requirements for Co-PIs?
qa_pairs.answer: A Co-PI is any Canadian faculty member with an Alliance Academic PI role who is actively involved in the computational project. The PI and all Co-PIs must upload an updated Canadian Common CV (CCV) with the application, and their role and involvement must be justified. A Collaborator is any other person actively participating in the project who cannot be listed as a Co-PI, such as international investigators or colleagues without an Alliance account; they can be listed in the Resource Management section of the application document.
